rag_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to implementation-level references in type annexes should be resolved by moving valid bindings to implementation scopes or removing invalid guarantees.
AGREE property associations must reference valid identifiers present in the current AADL scope; otherwise, remove the association.
AGREE contracts must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the analyzed model.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics reference unresolved identifiers, trace and fix the root cause or remove the invalid clause.
Implementation-level guarantees should only reference visible outputs or valid subcomponent paths in implementation scopes.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that compare Boolean or opaque data types using numeric expressions unless the AADL model supports such representations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in appropriate component scopes.
Property associations in AGREE must refer to valid AADL identifiers or features in the current scope.
anti_patterns: Do not add implementation-level bindings or references in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or variables.
Do not generate AGREE clauses that compare Boolean or opaque data types using integer expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about stack size properties
